messages.content:
You are an expert Technical Product Manager.
Your job is to analyze the user's issue (Title and Body) along with the provided source code context, and generate a User Story, Acceptance Criteria, and Tasks.

OUTPUT FORMAT REQUIREMENTS:
You MUST output ONLY a valid JSON object. Do not wrap it in markdown. Do not include any explanations outside of the JSON.
The JSON must strictly follow this structure:
{
  "title": "Short title of the story",
  "story_type": "software_feature", 
  "jira_issue_type": "Story",
  "jira_labels": ["backend", "frontend", "bug", etc],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a [role], I want to [action] so that [benefit].",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given [precondition] When [action] Then [result]"
  ],
  "story_points": null, // Hoáş·c má»™t sá»‘ nguyĂŞn Fibonacci (1, 2, 3, 5, 8) Ä‘Ăˇnh giĂˇ Ä‘á»™ khĂł
  "tasks": {
    "be": ["Backend task 1", "Backend task 2"],
    "fe": ["Frontend task 1"],
    "qa": ["Test case 1"]
  },
  "definition_of_done": ["Code reviewed", "Tests passed"],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [],
  "assumptions": [],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": []
}

If the issue lacks so much context that it's impossible to deduce the required changes even with the provided code context, you may set "planning_status" to "NEEDS_CLARIFICATION" and provide your questions in "clarification_questions", leaving "tasks" empty. Otherwise, strive to generate concrete tasks.

ISSUE TITLE: AgentHost: "Install & Watch" runs on existing sessions ISSUE BODY: - Start the agents window with a remote AH configured - Many instances of "Install & Watch" all start, causing high cpu/memory usage on the remote --- RAG CONTEXT (SOURCE CODE) --- --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- # Agent host unit tests For tests in this area that touch the SessionDatabase, they MUST use an in-memory database, not a real database file on disk. Use `SessionDatabase.open(':memory:')` and see the examples from existing tests. ===== FILE: D:\VSss-codedata\vscode\src\vs\platform\agentHost\test\auth-rework.md ===== --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- # Agent Host Sessions Provider **Folder:** `src/vs/sessions/contrib/providers/agentHost/` The agent host provider family backs sessions run by an **agent host** â€” an out-of-process (or in-process) agent runtime that exposes one or more agents (Copilot, Codex, Claude, â€¦) over the agent host protocol (`platform/agentHost`). It is the largest provider in the Agents window and is shared between the local window and remote hosts: | Class | File | Purpose | |-------|------|---------| | `BaseAgentHostSessionsProvider` | `browser/baseAgentHostSessionsProvider.ts` | Abstract base implementing the full `ISessionsProvider` surface against an `IAgentConnection`. ~2700 lines; contains `AgentHostSessionAdapter` (the `ISession` impl) and `NewSession` (pre-creation draft). | | `LocalAgentHostSessionsProvider` | `browser/localAgentHostSessionsProvider.ts` | Concrete local-window provider backed by the in-process `IAgentHostService`. | | `RemoteAgentHostSessionsProvider` | `../remoteAgentHost/` | Concrete remote provider (one per connection). Documented separately in [`REMOTE_AGENT_HOST_SESSIONS_PROVIDER.md`](../remoteAgentHost/REMOTE_AGENT_HOST_SESSIONS_PROVIDER.md). | This document covers the shared base and the **local** concrete provider. For the remote variant, read the remote doc â€” it extends the same base. --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- ## Extended Provider Interface Agent host providers implement `IAgentHostSessionsProvider` (defined in sessions core at `src/vs/sessions/common/agentHostSessionsProvider.ts`), which extends `ISessionsProvider` with: - **Remote connection members** (optional, populated only by remote providers): `connectionStatus`, `remoteAddress`, `connect()`, `disconnect()`, `canConnectOnDemand`. - **Dynamic session config**: `onDidChangeSessionConfig`, `getSessionConfig`, `isSessionConfigResolving`, `setSessionConfigValue`, `replaceSessionConfig`, `getSessionConfigCompletions`. These power the per-session configuration picker (isolation, branch, and other host-declared properties resolved live from the backend schema). `isAgentHostProvider(provider: ISessionsProvider)` (same file) is a type guard returning `true` for the local and remote agent host providers; `isAgentHostProviderId(providerId: string)` is the id-only variant, `true` for `local-agent-host` and any `agenthost-*` (remote) provider id. --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- ## `AgentSessionAdapter` â€” Wrapping Existing Sessions Adapts an existing `IAgentSession` from the chat layer into the `ISession` facade: - Constructs with initial values from the agent session's metadata and timing - `update(session)` performs a batched observable transaction to update all reactive properties - Extracts workspace info, changes, description, and GitHub info from session metadata - Maps `ChatSessionStatus` â†’ `SessionStatus` - Handles both CLI and Cloud session metadata formats for repository resolution --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- ## Local vs Remote Differences | Aspect | Local (`LocalAgentHostSessionsProvider`) | Remote (`RemoteAgentHostSessionsProvider`) | |--------|------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------| | Connection | In-process `IAgentHostService` (always present) | One live `IAgentConnection` per remote host | | Instances | One | One per connection (created/disposed dynamically) | | Resource scheme | `agent-host-${sessionType.id}` | `remote-${authority}-${agent.provider}` | | Browse actions | none | host-filesystem "Folders" picker | | Diff URIs | `toAgentHostUri(uri, 'local')` | host-scoped mapper | | Extra interface members | â€” | `connectionStatus`, `remoteAddress`, `connect`/`disconnect` |

response:
```json
{
  "title": "AgentHost: Prevent duplicate 'Install & Watch' sessions on existing remote connections",
  "story_type": "software_feature", 
  "jira_issue_type": "Story",
  "jira_labels": ["agent-host", "remote-sessions", "performance", "bug"],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a VS Code user, I want the 'Install & Watch' feature to check for existing active sessions on remote agent hosts before starting new ones, so that multiple instances of the Agents window don't create duplicate sessions and cause high CPU/memory usage.",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given an existing session is running on a remote agent host, When 'Install & Watch' is clicked from the Agents window, Then no new session should be started",
    "Given no session exists for a specific agent/provider combination on a remote host, When 'Install & Watch' is clicked, Then exactly one session should be created and started",
    "The 'Install & Watch' button should visually indicate when sessions already exist (e.g., disabled state or different icon)",
    "Existing running sessions must not be terminated by the 'Install & Watch' action",
    "Memory usage on remote hosts remains stable regardless of number of Agents windows open"
  ],
  "story_points": 5,
  "tasks": {
    "be": [
      "Add method to RemoteAgentHostSessionsProvider to query existing sessions from the remote agent host connection",
      "Implement session existence check logic in NewSession creation flow for remote providers",
      "Update session adapter to properly track active/inactive state when adapting existing IAgentSession",
      "Add configuration option or flag to control 'Install & Watch' behavior per provider"
    ],
    "fe": [
      "Update Agents window UI to show disabled/alternative icon for 'Install & Watch' when sessions already exist",
      "Add tooltip explaining why button is disabled (sessions already running)",
      "Show session count indicator next to 'Install & Watch' button showing existing active sessions"
    ],
    "qa": [
      "Test case: Verify no duplicate sessions created when clicking 'Install & Watch' on existing remote connection",
      "Test case: Verify exactly one session created for new agent/provider combination",
      "Test case: Verify memory usage remains stable with multiple Agents windows open",
      "Test case: Verify existing running sessions are not terminated by 'Install & Watch'"
    ]
  },
  "definition_of_done": [
    "Code reviewed and approved",
    "All unit tests passing (including SessionDatabase in-memory tests)",
    "Integration tests for remote agent host provider",
    "UI tests verifying button state changes based on session existence",
    "Performance regression test confirming memory usage is stable"
  ],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [
    "Should 'Install & Watch' create a new session even if an identical agent/provider already exists, or should it only start when no matching session exists?",
    "What defines two sessions as 'equivalent'? Same agent ID? Same provider? Same workspace?",
    "Should the check be performed on connection open vs. per button click?",
    "Is there a timeout mechanism needed - if a session was started but not fully initialized, should it count as existing?"
  ],
  "assumptions": [
    "The remote agent host protocol provides an API to query/list existing sessions",
    "Session identity can be uniquely determined by agent ID + provider combination",
    "Current implementation uses IAgentConnection for remote communication",
    "Memory leak is caused by multiple session instances being created rather than session objects not being cleaned up"
  ],
  "story_splits": [
    {
      "name": "Backend: Session existence check API",
      "description": "Implement queryExistingSessions method on RemoteAgentHostSessionsProvider to fetch active sessions from remote host"
    },
    {
      "name": "Frontend: UI state update",
      "description": "Update 'Install & Watch' button to show disabled state when equivalent sessions exist"
    }
  ],
  "sprint_allocation": [
    {"task": "Backend session query implementation", "points": 3, "assignee": ""},
    {"task": "Frontend UI update", "points": 2, "assignee": ""}
  ],
  "context_sources": [
    "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - Agent Host Sessions Provider documentation",
    "D:\\VSss-codedata\\vscode\\src\\vs\\platform\\agentHost\\test\\auth-rework.md - Test context"
  ]
}
```
